_note: Round-15 follow-up #1: the source-order walker `scan_statement_in_source_order` recorded literal-var aliases (`const EV = 'save'`) only when the binding kind was `const`. Upstream's `getVariableAtTopLevel` (`ComponentEvents.ts:339`) walks every top-level VariableDeclaration regardless of kind. Pre-fix native dropped `let EV = 'save'; dispatch(EV)` — the dispatched-name walker didn't see 'save' and the round-8 #5 duplicate-collapse pass kept the inline-typed dispatcher's `save: CustomEvent<number>`. Post-fix the let alias resolves the same way as const, the duplicate is detected, and the round-7 #5 untyped-override layer collapses 'save' to `CustomEvent<any>`. Inner here pairs `<{ save: number }>` with an untyped dispatcher whose `dispatch(EV)` resolves to 'save' via the let alias; Consumer's `(e: CustomEvent<string>) => void` handler is assignable post-fix and the file type-checks clean. Pre-fix the typed source's `CustomEvent<number>` survived and the wrong-typed handler fired TS2345.
